signals: AI tools generating UI components from prompts
Reduced need for simple page-building tasks

workflow_change.before: Spent most time building screens, styling components, and wiring basic interactions manually.
workflow_change.after: Uses AI to scaffold components and refine styling, with more time spent reviewing output and understanding product intent.

actions: Started learning UX reasoning and interaction patterns
Focused more on component architecture and state design
Asked to be included in architecture discussions to build visibility

outcome.early_effect: Basic UI work is faster, but expectations to understand product logic are higher.

narrative.summary: Simple frontend execution feels less differentiating than before. Value seems to be shifting toward judgment about user experience and system behavior.
